data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 3 AM ET: Bonds Rally, Stocks Slip After Inflation Data  | Supply ...
data.description:
Bonds Rally, Stocks Slip After Inflation Data

Treasury yields drop after CPI report shows tariffs aren't making prices spiral.

----

Supply Chains Become New Battleground in the Global Trade War

The U.S.-China talks on trade resemble arms-control negotiations, with export controls being the key weapons in each side's arsenal.

----

China Puts Six-Month Limit on Its Ease of Rare-Earth Export Licenses

A tentative deal reached in London gives Beijing continued leverage in trade negotiations.

----

U.K. Economy Shrinks on Tariff Hit

Production at the country's factories contracted more than expected in April as evolving U.S. trade policy brought a halt to a surprising surge in activity.

----

Inside the Race to Cash in on Fannie and Freddie

Investors, mortgage lenders, and the government all see an angle. How it ends isn't clear.

----

Bond Market Prepares for Big Treasury Auction to Settle Demand Doubts

The U.S. Treasury Department is selling $22 billion worth of bonds maturing in 30 years on Thursday.

----

Muted May Inflation Defies Tariff Fears

Consumer prices rose 2.4% over the year, and the month-over-month increase was lower than expected.

----

U.S. Ambassador to Canada Says Breakthrough Possible in Bilateral Talks at G-7 Summit

U.S. Ambassador to Canada Pete Hoekstra said that all indications point to the possibility of a good economic-and-security deal for both the U.S. and Canada.

----

EPA to Repeal Air-Pollution Standards on Power Plants

The proposals would roll back Biden-era climate rules and Obama-era mercury limits.

----

U.S. Crude-Oil Stockpiles Down More Than Expected

U.S. crude-oil inventories fell last week by 3.6 million barrels, surpassing an expected decline of 1.6 million-barrels.

----

China's Lock on Rare Earths Dictated Path Toward Trade Truce

Control of minerals used in electronics has given Beijing powerful leverage in trade negotiations with the U.S.

----

Texas Governor to Deploy National Guard as Anti-ICE Protests Spread

Arrests grow as more cities plan demonstrations after the unrest in Los Angeles.

----

Stocks Can Break Record Highs if These Things Go Right. That's a Big If.

An extension of the 2017 tax cuts, trade deals, and rate cuts would all send stocks to record highs, says Bellwether Wealth. It's a big ask, however.

----

ECB's Rate Cut Helps Ensure Inflation Won't Settle Below Target, Lane Says

The European Central Bank last week lowered its key interest rate after figures showed the annual rate of inflation fell to 1.9% in May.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Company Headlines at 3 AM ET: Oracle Shares Jump as CEO Targets 'Dramatically Higher' Revenue Growth | Tesco ...
data.description:
Oracle Shares Jump as CEO Targets 'Dramatically Higher' Revenue Growth

The cloud-services company logged double-digit revenue growth in the fourth quarter and set its sights on even bigger gains in the fiscal year ahead, pushing shares higher after hours.

----

Tesco Sales Rise as Market Share Increases Despite Tough Competition

The U.K. grocer maintained its full-year expectations after like-for-like sales in its home market rose 5.1%, and edged up 4.1% in central Europe.

----

Voyager Technologies Rises in Debut, Signaling Improving IPO Market

Shares of space- and defense-technology company Voyager Technologies climbed in their New York Stock Exchange debut, another sign of an improving market for initial public offerings.

----

Chime Prices IPO Above Range, Valuing It at $9.8 Billion. The Stock Starts Trading Tomorrow.

Chime, a digital bank popular with younger customers, is the latest tech firm to go public. The stock will begin trading Thursday under the ticker symbol CHYM.

----

How Amazon's Nuclear Deal Could Solve a Touchy Political Problem

The data-center agreement with Talen Energy could help ease fears of rising electricity prices for homeowners.

----

Bojangles Is Exploring a Sale While the Fried-Chicken Market Is Hot

A transaction could fetch upward of $1.5 billion, roughly triple what it sold for in 2019.

----

Allison Transmission to Buy Dana's Off-Highway Business for About $2.7 Billion

Allison said the deal will allow it to offer a wider range of commercial-duty powertrain and industrial solutions .

----

At Home, Facing Debt and Tariffs, Plans to Close Some Stores in Bankruptcy

The home-goods retailer, owned by private-equity firm Hellman & Friedman, is expected to file for bankruptcy protection within days.

----

Auto-Parts Bankruptcy Is the First Big Casualty of Tariff War

KKR's Marelli blames tariffs and lingering supply-chain issues stemming from the Covid-19 pandemic for its descent into chapter 11.

----

Howard Schultz Backs Starbucks CEO Brian Niccol's Turnaround Approach

The influential former chief says doubters of the coffee chain's comeback plan will be proven wrong.

----

Maker of Tommy Bahama says consumer caution is affecting 'fundamentally everything we sell'

Oxford Industries Inc. - the company that owns the Tommy Bahama and Lilly Pulitzer beach- and resort-wear lines - slashed its full-year profit outlook on Wednesday, sending shares lower in after-hours trade, as U.S. tariffs "significantly" complicate business.

----

Chewy Shares Decline as Solid Quarter Trumped by Lofty Expectations

Chewy executives said that the company continues to expect a minimal impact from tariffs.

----

Disney, Universal Sue Midjourney Over AI-Generated Princesses and Minions

Disney and Universal sued Midjourney, alleging that the artificial-intelligence company's image-generating service produces unauthorized copies of the studios' copyrighted works.

----

Nvidia and Perplexity Team Up in European AI Push

The chip giant and AI search startup are offering specialized AI models for European users, aiming to widen their reach in the region.

----

Fears Over U.S. AI Dominance Boost Business for France's Mistral

The AI developer's CEO said European customers were seeking independence from Silicon Valley.

----

These Diabetes Drugs Are Finding New Life as an Antiaging Hack

Despite incomplete evidence, longevity enthusiasts are experimenting with SGLT2 inhibitors in their quest to live healthier for longer.

----

Airbus Expects Air Traffic Growth to Fuel Demand for New Planes

The plane manufacturer said that the growth in air travel will require 43,400 new passenger and freighter aircraft deliveries over the next 20 years.

data.title: Instalco wins assignment at Skanska’s new head office
data.description:
The Instalco subsidiary, Ohmegi Elektro AB, has been contracted for the electrical and telecom installations at a new office building called Olivin, located on Kungholmen, Stockholm. The building will, among others, serve as the new head office for Skanska and the order value for Instalco is approximately SEK 50 million.

Olivin is a new office complex that is being built in the Lindhagen district of Västra Kungsholmen in Stockholm. Upon completion, the building will comprise 11 floors and offer 23,000 square metres of lettable space, characterized by a striking triangular design.

“We have a good relationship with Skanska and many years of experience collaborating with them on projects like this. At Olivin, the work will entail a great deal of reused materials and alternative products, according to the customer’s requirements,” says Johan Brodin, CEO at Ohmegi Elektro.

The project has recently started for Ohmegi Elektro, and it is expected to be completed towards the end of 2026. Other key objectives are to achieve LEED Platinum certification, along with NollCO2 certification issued by the Sweden Green Building Council. Another goal is to certify the project as a Sustainable Instalco Project.

Due to its structural complexity and demanding timeline, this assignment will require meticulous planning and delivery coordination, with successful collaboration among all parties being crucial. A strong emphasis will be placed on innovative approaches to sustainability and reuse, aiming to stay at the leading edge of development in these fields.

For more information, please contact:

Fredrik Trahn, Head of Communications & Sustainability, fredrik.trahn@instalco.se, +46-70-913 67 96

SAVILLS PLC (THE "COMPANY")

ALLOCATION OF SHARES UNDER THE SAVILLS SHARE INCENTIVE PLAN

NOTIFICATION OF TRANSACTIONS BY PERSONS DISCHARGING MANAGERIAL RESPONSIBILITIES AND PERSONS CLOSELY ASSOCIATED WITH THEM

The Savills Share Incentive Plan ("the Plan") is a share purchase plan available to all employees of participating companies and Executive Directors/ PDMRs are eligible to participate.  Each month the Trustees of the Plan invest participants' contributions in Savills plc ordinary shares of 2.5p each ("Ordinary Shares") at the prevailing market price in accordance with the Plan Rules.

data.title: AIC Mines Finds High-Grade Copper Mineralization at Queensland Project
data.description:
AIC Mines found high-grade copper mineralization during recent drilling at the Jericho copper deposit within its Eloise copper project in Queensland, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The results include 5.5 meters grading 2.4% copper from 696 meters.

The company said the result confirms that high-grade mineralization continues at depth, several hundred meters below the current inferred resources.

data.title: U.K.'s Foundering Economy Is Trouble for Govt's Spending Plans — Market Talk
data.description: A backsliding U.K. economy spells bad news for a government dependent on growth to fund its spending plans, MHA economic adviser Joe Nellis says. Britain's GDP fell by 0.3% in April, figures show Thursday, ending the first quarter's surprise surge in activity and output. That isn't ideal for government treasury chief Rachel Reeves, who a day earlier detailed billions of pounds in spending on housing, digital infrastructure and defense, Nellis writes in a note to clients. "The sustainability of her spending promises is critically dependent on changing this, kickstarting the economy to grow GDP and collect more revenue through tax receipts," he says. Otherwise, Reeves may be forced into fresh tax increases later this year, Nellis adds. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

data.title: Tariff Debate Weighs Heavily on the Dollar — Market Talk
data.description: The tariff debate is weighing heavily on the U.S. dollar, says Felix Brill, Chief Investment Officer at VP Bank. Investors are favoring European investments instead, which is helping the euro. Following the dollar's significant losses, technical analysis is signalling a consolidation. The trade-weighted U.S. dollar is significantly overvalued and susceptible to further losses in view of the high U.S. trade deficit. This deficit goes hand in hand with a record level of foreign debt. The higher the foreign liabilities, the more difficult it becomes to receive additional financing. "Correcting this imbalance requires more attractive U.S. asset prices, which is primarily achieved by devaluing the dollar," Brill says. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

data.title: Eni Shares Up 1.35% On Oil Price Rise
NABARD accepts bids for 2028 bond reissue, bankers say
data.description:
India's National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD) has accepted bids worth 44.03 billion Indian rupees for the reissue of 7.48% September 2028 bonds, three bankers said on Thursday.

NABARD will offer a yield of 6.68% on this issue and had invited bids from bankers and investors earlier in the day, they said.

The state-owned company did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment.

data.title: Australian shares erase early gains to close lower
data.description:
Key points:
  • ASX 200 snaps two sessions of record gains
  • Middle East tensions curb risk appetite
  • Traders await further details on US-China talks
  • Energy, gold stocks rise

Australian shares gave up early advances to close lower on Thursday, as investors booked profits after encouraging cues from U.S.-China trade talks fuelled two sessions of record gains.

The S&P/ASX 200 index ended 0.3% lower at 8,565.1 points after rising as much as 0.3% early in the session. The benchmark closed at record highs in the previous two sessions.

Markets globally were closely monitoring the U.S.-China trade talks this week, which resulted in a framework agreement that would remove Chinese export restrictions on rare earth minerals and allow Chinese students access to U.S. universities.

However, the United States saying it was readying a partial evacuation of its Iraqi embassy due to heightened security risks in the region proved a dampener for risk appetite.

Back in Sydney, heavyweight financial stocks ended 0.5% lower, dragged down by the 'Big Four' banks, which fell between 0.2% and 0.8%.

Australia's big lenders, often seen as the backbone of the economy, have seen a robust rally recently on expectations of lower near-term rates.

Banking stocks, which have been beneficiaries from the last two rate cuts and the one expected in July, are naturally likelier candidates for any retracement or profit taking, said Junvum Kim, Asia Pacific senior sales trader at Saxo Markets.

Energy stocks ended flat. The sub-index rose as much as 2.3% to hit its highest level since March 4 in early trade, following a jump in oil prices on escalating U.S.-Iran tensions.

Gold stocks climbed 2.6%, limiting overall losses, as bullion prices firmed on escalating geopolitical tensions and a weaker dollar, while softer U.S. inflation data boosted expectations of Federal Reserve rate cuts.

New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index rose 0.3% to finish the session at 12,649.1.

data.title: Australian Shares Inch Down Amid Trump's Tariff Plans; Monash IVF Group CEO Resigns After Second Embryo Error
data.description:
Australian shares inched lower at Thursday's close after US President Donald Trump declared plans to notify trading partners of new unilateral tariff rates.

The S&P/ASX 200 Index fell 0.3% or 27 points to close at 8,565.1.

Trump plans to notify trading partners within two weeks of an intent to reinstate higher duties ahead of a July 9 deadline, Bloomberg reported.

However, the President is known for setting two-week deadlines that are often postponed, or never materialize at all, the report added.

"Common sense would suggest this is another Trump strategy to increase urgency in trade negotiations," said Rodrigo Catril, senior foreign exchange strategist at National Australia Bank.

On the domestic front, Commonwealth Bank of Australia's Household Spending Insights Index rose 0.5% in May, indicating early signs of consumer recovery as reduced petrol and electricity costs boost spending on discretionary items, although overall growth remains cautious amid economic uncertainty, according to a report by the Bank.

In company news, Monash IVF Group said Michael Knaap resigned as Chief Executive, following a second embryo transfer error by the company disclosed on Tuesday. Shares of the company rose 9% at market close.

Cettire reported fiscal year-to-date sales revenue of AU$693.8 million until May 31, up nearly 2% from AU$682.2 million in the previous comparable period. Shares of the company fell 31% at market close and earlier hit an all-time low.

Lastly, Qantas Airways may see margin expansion due to lower fuel prices, while demand conditions are expected to be stable and fares rise, reported the Australian Financial Review. Shares of the company rose past 2% at market close.

data.title: German medical tech firm Brainlab plans Frankfurt IPO in coming weeks
data.description:
German medical technology firm Brainlab is planning an initial public offering on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange in the coming weeks, the company said on Thursday.

The Munich-based company said it will list on the regulated market and aims to raise up to 200 million euros ($230 million) through the issuance of new shares.

The firm will use the capital increase to grow its business, with a focus on expanding into additional clinical applications, as well as strengthen its balance sheet.

The current owners also plan to place additional secondary shares to ensure significant free float, said the company.

Majority shareholder Stefan Vilsmeier, who founded the company 35 years ago and recently announced his retirement from the operational business, is set to continue to support the company as a long-term investor.

The initial public offering is being organised by Berenberg, Deutsche Bank, Commerzbank, Jefferies, and UniCredit.

Evercore is also advising the company, which has around 2,000 employees.

Brainlab's main products include software systems that are used as navigational tools by surgeons, for example during brain and hip surgery, and for spinal implants and fractures.

($1 = 0.8678 euros)

data.title: Kenyan shilling slightly firmer in slow trade
data.description:
Kenya's shilling was slightly stronger on Thursday in slow trade, traders said.

At 0703 GMT commercial banks quoted the shilling at 128.90/129.40 per dollar, compared with Wednesday's close of 129.00/129.50.

data.title: Italy Extends Probe on Sinochem's Alleged Influence Over Pirelli
data.description:
Italian authorities extended the deadline to decide on a probe into allegations that Pirelli's largest shareholder, Sinochem , broke regulations limiting Chinese influence on the tire maker, Bloomberg News reported Thursday, citing documents seen by the news outlets.

The government will release the ruling on July 31 following a 60-day extension due to the "considerable amount of information" needing investigation, the report said.

The new deadline could spark tensions with Beijing and hurt other Italian firms with Chinese stakeholdings or exposure, the report said.

Italy also faces pressure from the US to limit Sinochem's role in Pirelli, Bloomberg said.

The Italian government started investigating Pirelli in November, Bloomberg reported.

Sinochem, Pirelli, and Prime Minister Meloni's office did not immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

data.title: Japan to conduct auction for tariff-free rice earlier than usual
data.description:
Japan will hold a tender for tariff-free imported staple rice this month, earlier than the usual auction in September, to help lower soaring prices, Agriculture Minister Shinjiro Koizumi said in an X social media post on Thursday.

The rice is expected to be delivered by late September instead of December in a typical year, Koizumi said.

Higher rice prices have become a serious concern for consumers and policymakers ahead of key elections in Tokyo and the parliament's upper house.

The government has been releasing rice from its emergency stockpile in an effort to stabilise prices, and consumers have been standing in long queues to buy it at less than half averages prices.

About 800,000 metric tons out of the 900,000-ton stockpile has already been earmarked for sale by August, and Koizumi had said the government would utilise imports if needed to keep supplying the grain to the market.

Japan imports up to 100,000 tons of staple rice a year tariff-free under the "minimum access" framework agreed within the World Trade Organization.

data.title: Market Chatter: Jollibee Foods to Buy South Korea's Norang Food by End-2025
data.description:
Philippines-based fast food chain Jollibee Foods will acquire South Korean chicken franchise operator Norang Food by the end of this year, Pulse News reported Wednesday.

Seoul-based private equity firms Corstone Asia and Q Capital Partners selected Jollibee as the preferred bidder and signed a memorandum of understanding on Tuesday. The deal, led by Jollibee's Korean subsidiary, is valued in the mid-100 billion won range, the report said.

Due diligence is expected to conclude by August, with the purchase agreement expected by end-2025, according to the report.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Shanghai Composite Index Ends Flat at 3402.66
Brainlab to Raise Around $230 Million in Frankfurt Listing
data.description:
(06:25 GMT) *Brainlab Plans IPO on Frankfurt Stock Exchange
(06:25 GMT) *Brainlab: Listing to Take Place in Coming Weeks
(06:26 GMT) *Brainlab Plans Capital Increase of Up to EUR200 Million Associated With IPO
(06:28 GMT) *Brainlab: Placement of Additional Secondary Shares Will Ensure Significant Free Float
(06:28 GMT) *Brainlab: Majority Shareholder Stefan Vilsmeier Will Continue to Support Company
(06:29 GMT) *Brainlab: IPO Funds Should Support Commercialization of Workflows, Expansion
(07:03 GMT) Brainlab to Raise Around $230 Million in Frankfurt Listing

By Andrea Figueras

German medical-technology company Brainlab plans to launch an initial public offering on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange and raise 200 million euros ($229.8 million) as it seeks to foster its next phase of growth.

The company said Thursday that the IPO would take place in the coming weeks. The move is expected to drive commercialization, support expansion into adjacent clinical domains and strengthen the balance sheet.

Current majority shareholder Stefan Vilsmeier will continue to support the company as a long-term investor, while the placement of additional secondary shares will ensure significant free float and sufficient trading liquidity, the group said.

Brainlab, its management, and major shareholders plan to enter into customary lock-up agreements.

"We currently address only a fraction of the clinical areas that our technology platform could support," Chief Executive Rainer Birkenbach said. The group plans to grow in additional clinical domains through a pipeline of new products, some already available, others in development.

For the first half of fiscal 2025, the group booked revenue of 239 million euros. Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization amounted to 54 million euros, with a margin of 22.4%.

Brainlab aims for mid-term revenue growth of between 10% and 13%, with additional upside potential. It also expects an Ebitda margin in the mid-20s, with a long-term ambition of up to 30%.

Write to Andrea Figueras at andrea.figueras@wsj.com

data.title: OCBC Buys Back Shares Worth Over SG$6 Million
data.description:
Oversea-Chinese Banking Corp. or OCBC bought back 400,000 shares in the open market on Wednesday for SG$6.5 million, or SG$16.15 a piece, a filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The bank has been authorized to buy back 224.9 million shares under its current mandate. To date, it has repurchased 1.1 million shares.

data.title: Pound Holds Near 3-Year Highs
data.description:
The British pound hovered around $1.35, near its highest level in three years, supported by broad-based dollar weakness amid renewed tariff threats from President Trump and signs of cooling US inflation.

In the UK, the GDP contracted by 0.3% in April, significantly more than the anticipated 0.1% decline.

The downturn was driven by a combination of higher energy bills, increased National Insurance contributions, and elevated Stamp Duty Land Tax rates.

Labour market indicators also pointed to a loss of momentum.

Despite these headwinds, the Bank of England is widely expected to hold interest rates steady next week.

Meanwhile, Chancellor Reeves unveiled a multi-year spending review, committing £2 trillion in public expenditures.

The plan includes annual departmental budget increases of 2.3% in real terms, signaling continued support for key sectors.

However, analysts highlighted the limited fiscal space outlined in the review, raising the likelihood of tax increases later this year.
